-- 56 of 146 --

Depesh
Mandalia
Depesh
Mandalia
- Mine for winning products. But requires testing budget. No more than $100-200 per product
- Facebook users respond better to specifics
- “You’ll love these new surround sound headphones” (Appeals to everyone & no one)
vs
- “As a gamer immerse yourself in the crisp 360 degree surround sound headphones” (Will
appeal to the gamers that want a full spectrum of sound)
- Grouping audiences and products together
- STAR Method of testing: Setup - Test - Analyse - Repeat
- Only need ONE PRODUCT to scale to 7-figures!
Product Mining Strategy
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Mandalia
Fast Setup - Fast Failure. $5/d/ad set.
Split audiences into separate ad sets, minimise variables > PPE
Can group interests, but can’t unpick individually within FB
No more than one interest or behaviour, or interest/behaviour pair, or LLA or CA
2 Creative angles (example: health & nutrition > wellbeing, weightloss, body
building)
Graduate winners into main campaign to test performance
THEN combine interests to support scaling
Setup
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Depesh
Mandalia
Depesh
Mandalia
- Focussed on ads only (best audience) - combinations of format, copy, visual, CTA can be tested
- Mainly PPE, can be LG, VV, WC, PC etc
- Use your best audience so you know the only variable is creative
- $5+ per ad set - 3 ads per ad set/1 per ad set/Split Test tool
- If using PPE/VV leave original running share post ID to rack up proof
- STAR Method of testing: Setup - Test - Analyse - Repeat
Creative Fishing Strategy
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 82 of 146 --

Depesh
Mandalia
Depesh
Mandalia
3N Ads Formula + 5W PERSONA + MASLOW
- STOP - ENGAGE - ACTION (3 nudges)
- Understand your avatar - walk in their shoes, stalk them online, what words do they use, what
visuals do they respond to - 5W PERSONA
- Write copy and select creative for avatar
- Test one format/persona at a time
- Remove emotion and guesswork!
- First 500 impressions matter; sometimes a duplication works
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 96 of 146 --

Depesh
Mandalia
Depesh
Mandalia
Testing
1. Data & Insights - setup the right reports in Ads Manager based on your objectives. For testing focus on CPC and
CTR when using PPE, but also look at metrics further down the funnel (product views, sign up page views, webinar
views, adds to cart, purchase, sign up etc). For video testing track % 10 second views and cost per 10s view (below
$0.10)
2. Document learnings - Grab the Trello Growth board here: http://bit.ly/DM-Trello-Growth-FB
3. Use the list here http://bit.ly/DM-FBTreats to test creative apps and techniques
4. Give enough time and money, but cut early
5. Fail forward; mindset of learning, not failure
6. Fast failure
7. Ongoing:
a. Test one new audience at least every fortnight
b. Test at LEAST one new creative every week - more so as you scale
8. Have creatives queued up, tested, ready to hit your main campaigns
9. The higher the ticket price, the longer the journey, the better the story telling needs to be
10. Remove emotion from testing! Use a data-driven approach but don’t be afraid to tweak and retest creatives
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 110 of 146 --

Depesh
Mandalia
Depesh
Mandalia
Landing Page performance is a big factor towards relevance score & auction cost
That includes the technicalities too. Tips from Facebook:
> Minimize landing page redirects, plugins, and link shorteners and compress large files
> Utilize multi-region hosting to improve server response time
>> Use a high-quality Content Delivery Network (CDN) to reach audiences quickly
>>> Remove render-blocking javascript
Use https://tools.pingdom.com (free) to find areas for optimization
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Golden Scaling Automation
WHEN: At 9am ONLY every morning
Increase budget 20% if the last 3 days ROAS has been within target and you’ve made at least 10
acquisitions (ROAS takes into account CPA and AOV)

Golden Money Saver Rule
RULE 1
WHEN: From 1pm-5pm
Pause ads if ROAS today is lower than my target + 25%
RULE 2
WHEN: 5pm-1159pm
Pause ads if ROAS today is lower than my target + 10%

Depesh
Mandalia
Depesh
Mandalia
Foundations
1. Account setup
a. Don’t run ads from your personal account, go straight into a Business Manager
b. Create a second business manager on your profile. Know that FB will ban you for multiple profiles, but you can ask a friend or business partner to also create BMs.
Be safe!
c. Create 5 accounts, start with 2 accounts live min for each store. Leave your pixel and audience data in your ‘Master’ account (don’t run ads here) and use Account 2
for all your activity. Policy is stricter than it’s ever been even pros are not immune to it
d. If you plan to scale hard, then be prepared to have an account review if you scale too fast (anti-fraud measures). Also be prepared to hit your account spend limits.
Keep a very close eye especially for example in Q4 if you find a break out winner.
e. User credit cards with cashback but also be prepared for anti-fraud measures to kick-in here too - FB will debit your account on a regular but random basis. Keep
your card issuer informed. You can move to account credit and bank payment but this requires a business audit and established accounts. Something worth looking
at for Ecom stores with at least 12 months operation and consistent 5-figure billing
f. Ensure you prettify your Page. Don’t underestimate the role your page plays; doesn’t have to take long https://www.facebook.com/business/help/366099230478737/
i. Choose a good thumbnail image as it is one of the first things people see in ads
ii. A Page banner or video that gets your proposition out, don’t waste this. Setup category, contact details, about us and fill out some basic posts with photos
iii. Setup instant reply for Messenger to give a nice welcome message
g. If you're running multiple products, setup your catalog feed and be ready to use DPA
i. Shopify, Woocommerce, BigCommerce, Magento will push catalog data in else set it up manually or via 3rd party tool
https://www.facebook.com/business/help/1275400645914358
h.
with no clicks, or key events, learn and use. Spend less time on tracking more on action
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 141 of 146 --

Depesh
Mandalia
Depesh
Mandalia
Foundations
1. Pixel
a. ALWAYS double check it’s all working using Chrome’s Pixel Helper regardless of platform
i. VC > Product Page (including product details for DPA)
ii. ATC > On button click if possible (+ DPA)
iii. IC > Start checkout
iv. PI > Payment Page
v. Purchase Complete > Order complete page (Value, currency, Qty)
b. Use custom conversions
i. Eg ViewCart > /cart page (Custom conversion)
c. Use Events manager for trouble shooting eg FB not showing sales, Shopify is or vice versa or to ensure attributes are being passed
d. Use schema.org markup on your site (not all Ecom stores support this out of the box) - helps FB understand context and match users
e. Create 1% audiences of each of your events to test. If 1% doesn’t work, unlikely 2% + will especially in large countries like US
f. It’s ok to overaly interests on top of LLAs for testing if you don’t think your core custom audience is well defined
g. Good pixel setup is the core to your success
2. Reporting
a. Setup your funnel metrics in Ads Manager (screen). Save as a default view (you can set others up in future)
b. Export to GSheets for better conversion analysis (eg LP > VC > ATC > IC > Purch). Know your CR%s, by campaign, by ad set, by ad - use Supermetrics to pull data
automatically especially if running big spends and/or multiple accounts. Don’t underestimate the cost/benefit of time saved
c. Setup alerts using FB rules - especially for spends. Set thresholds eg if spend > £5 and zero clicks or spend > CPA x 3. At the early stage, use alerts and manual
actions until you’ve found the right threshold to apply automated rules
d. CPA calculator. Know what your CPC/CR% should be. Which is easier to manipulate?
e. ROAS calculator: Know your CPA goal with AOV to hit ROAS. Which is easier to manipulate?
f. Get to know Delivery insights: audience overlap, competition and saturation are the key areas to watch
g. Facebook analytics for deeper stuff but not worth worrying about until you’re doing decent 5-figure monthly revenues or above. Can unlock additional data on cohort
behaviours and channel mix
h. Use GA - tag your ads with: campaign name, ad set name, ad name where
i. Campaign name should include objective, country, product, funnel step
ii. ad set name should include age, gender, placement, device, location, targeting
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

1. FB ads for Ecom - Account Setup & Billing
a. Don’t run ads from your personal account, go straight into a Business Manager
b. Create a second business manager on your profile. Know that FB will ban you for multiple profiles, but you can ask a friend or business partner to also create BMs.
Be safe!
c. Create 5 accounts, start with 2 accounts live min for each store. Leave your pixel and audience data in your ‘Master’ account (don’t run ads here) and use Account 2
for all your activity. Policy is stricter than it’s ever been even pros are not immune to it
d. If you plan to scale hard, then be prepared to have an account review if you scale too fast (anti-fraud measures). Also be prepared to hit your account spend limits.
Keep a very close eye especially for example in Q4 if you find a break out winner.
e. User credit cards with cashback but also be prepared for anti-fraud measures to kick-in here too - FB will debit your account on a regular but random basis. Keep
your card issuer informed. You can move to account credit and bank payment but this requires a business audit and established accounts. Something worth looking
at for Ecom stores with at least 12 months operation and consistent 5-figure billing
f. Ensure you prettify your Page. Don’t underestimate the role your page plays; doesn’t have to take long https://www.facebook.com/business/help/366099230478737/
i. Choose a good thumbnail image as it is one of the first things people see in ads
ii. A Page banner or video that gets your proposition out, don’t waste this. Setup category, contact details, about us and fill out some basic posts with photos
iii. Setup instant reply for Messenger to give a nice welcome message
g. If you're running multiple products, setup your catalog feed and be ready to use DPA
i. Shopify, Woocommerce, BigCommerce, Magento will push catalog data in else set it up manually or via 3rd party tool
https://www.facebook.com/business/help/1275400645914358
2. FB ads for Ecom - Test & Analyse
a. The Graduation Framework
i. Using PPE, small budgets and fast iteration to find winners
ii. It works because it aligns to what FB wants: relevance and engagement for users. FB rewards you for a great user experience. Let that marinate in your
mind as you think about the ads and experiences you’re sharing
iii. Research your audience: Who, What, Why, When, Where
iv. Research your niche
v. Understand bidding is your outcome vs FB’s view of whether the user will like your desired outcome (ad, targeting, landing page etc)
vi. Find the best blend of audience + creative, fast
vii. 3N Ads formula
b. KPIs
c. Audiences & Objectives
d. Split Testing
3.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

1. FB ads for Ecom - Optimise
a. 4 Funnel System
b. Ads Sniper & Grad Framework
c. DPA for cold, schema/markup
d. Typical retargeting setup
e. Post purchase sequence + messenger
f. Sales Funnel Optimisation
i. Hotjar
ii. GA
iii. UserTesting
iv. 5SecondTest
v. Pingdom
vi. Mobile-first
vii. AB Testing
g. Page engagement
2. FB ads for Ecom - Scaling
a. Mindset
b. Oceans, boats and hooks
c. V-Scaling
d. H-Scaling
e. Campaign Optimized Budgets
f. Lifetime budgets
g. Manual bid; auto v target v lowest cost
h. Automation
3. Pitfalls
a. Not enough creative throughput
b. Too frequent changes (pacing algorithm)
c. Not enough conversions per ad set
d. Wrong metrics to optimise (eg break-even point, margins, LTV)
e. Bad website experience (UX, speed, bugs)
f. Audience overlap
g. Audience saturation
h. High competition
4.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 144 of 146 --

Depesh
Mandalia
Depesh
Mandalia
1. Multi-channel hacks
a. Test email lead gen to push into your email funnel if AOV > $100.
b. Test FB Lives to build up TOF awareness and engagement
c. Keep your Page active and engaged to bring CPM costs down
2. Cheaper traffic isn’t always the best traffic
a. Facebook’s an auction and better customers generally cost more
b. Traffic cost also relies on competition, your bidding strategy, seasonality and user experience
c. Winning the auction isn’t always the right thing to do!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 145 of 146 --

Depesh
Mandalia
Depesh
Mandalia
Foundations
1. Be in control of your data
2. Continuously test your avatar
3. Document testing - Grab the Trello Growth board here: http://bit.ly/DM-Trello-Growth-FB
4. Test one new audience every fortnight
5. Test at LEAST one new creative every week
6. Cut ads that are not working (Sniper Strategy)
7. Have creatives queued up, tested, ready to hit your main campaigns
8. Consider copywriting support - think more as a storyteller
9. Use the list here http://bit.ly/DM-FBTreats to test creative apps and techniques
10. Remove emotion from testing
11. Test landing pages
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
